programmes.show_description: Exactly the Wrong Time to Get Proud: Feeling the pressure of a beefed-up police presence, the Hop Wei try to survive. The Long Zii gain strong connections. (S3, ep 1 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following Britain's police. From the pursuit of a drink driver to an escalating domestic dispute, cops in west Wales have their work cut out. (S1, ep 12)
Missing Teeth: A marijuana user crashes his motorbike into the back of a car, a learner finds his car is too much to handle and a mishap causes a man to lose his teeth. (S12, ep 3)
Undies. Seatbelt. Simple: A driverless car crashes into a house, a drink driver is pulled over and a speeder accepts his fine with a bit of humour. (S12, ep 4)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 7)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 8)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 1)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S8, ep 2)
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they pull over motorists for a variety of suspected offences. (S15, ep 9)
Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they pull over motorists for a variety of suspected offences. (S15, ep 10)
At O'Hare International, a returning American's bananas cause a slip-up in secondary. A mother covers for her kid at Detroit's Ambassador Bridge. (S1, ep 26)
Officers discover a traveller is driving without a license and a US resident returns from the Dominican Republic with an unusual package. (S1, ep 27)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists. (S4, ep 7)

Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 5)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 6)
In Port Huron, a strong odour leads to a secondary for two Americans and an officer discovers gold at Chicago O'Hare Airport. (S1, ep 17)
CBP officers question a Mexican national with odd travel plans and in Port Huron, a traveller's past may come back to haunt him. (S1, ep 18)
A British national plans his very own Brexit. A package of happiness arrives at the mail centre, and Customs go on a boat-hunt in the Bay of Islands. (S11, ep 3)
An American woman attempts to smuggle a live cat across the border, and Customs find a soft toy stuffed with more than just stuffing... (S11, ep 4)

Necessary Evil: An attack on Quark's life brings Odo face-to-face with a five-year-old unsolved murder - for which Kira was a prime suspect.
Divide and Conquer: SG-1 fear that they may have fallen victim to an untraceable Goa'uld method of mind control. (S4, ep 5)
Workforce: This two parter concludes as Chakotay tries to convince Janeway and co of their past lives on Voyager. Problems arise when he's taken in for interrogation. (S7, ep 17)
The Battle: Captain Picard is accused of firing on an innocent Ferengi vessel in a battle that took place nine years ago.
A woman shocks the team with an incredible revelation as her bags are searched when a drug detector dog raises the alarm. A nervous passenger attracts attention. (S15, ep 5)
The contents of a young woman from China's suitcase could have catastrophic results for an entire industry. Two brothers claiming to be tourists are caught out in a lie. (S15, ep 6)
Follow-Up Day: Each unit is tasked with following up on dormant cases in the hope of discovering new leads. Meanwhile, Nolan gets some life-changing news. (S2, ep 13)
Casualties: National security issues arise as Officers Nolan and Harper investigate a murder. (S2, ep 14)
Fight Or Flight: The crew comes across an abandoned ship of dead aliens who seem to have been the victims of scientific experiments.
Anything Short of a Blow to the Head: Ah Sahm and Young Jun receive advice about producing authentic bills. The Secret Service tracks counterfeit money. (S3, ep 2 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 1)
Pie and Sauce: The driver of his car is spoken to by his lack of plates, a man tests positive for alcohol and meth use and Highway Patrol's biggest fan is pulled over. (S12, ep 5)
Big Trouble: Officers pull over a duo who have swapped seats to skirt the law, a bright yellow car highlights a dangerous driver and a party-goer has his plans put on hold. (S12, ep 6)

A man walking on the motorway inadvertently leads police straight to a bust and a sheep runs amok. (S16, ep 1)
The police get way more than they bargain for when they pursue a fleeing driver, then meet one of the drunkest men you'll ever see. (S16, ep 2)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a Japanese traveller must make a difficult decision. An officer discovers something seedy at the mail facility in Chicago. (S1, ep 28)
A hostile traveller spits out her true intentions, while a farmer's choice of crop makes him inadmissible. Plus, mail room officers smell a rat. (S2, ep 1)

An officer suspects a US resident returning from the Caribbean may be hiding something and two construction workers wind up at the border by mistake. (S1, ep 19)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a pregnant Chinese national has CBP asking questions and a security breach on the tarmac leads officers to a jet bound for Haiti. (S1, ep 20)
Immigration deal with a Singaporean national who claims to be meeting a mystery man. Plus, a flood in the biosecurity zone shuts down the airport. (S11, ep 5)
Documentary series following the men and woman who work to keep New Zealand's borders safe. (S11, ep 6)

Second Sight: Sisko falls in love for the first time since his wife's death, but the object of his affections may not be all that she seems.
Window of Opportunity: During one of the funniest episodes ever made, Jack O'Neill and Teal'c relive the same day over and over after being exposed to a strange alien device. (S4, ep 6)
Human Error: Seven of Nine tries to perfect her social skills on the holodeck, but her hobby becomes an obsession and she starts to neglect her duties. (S7, ep 18)
Hide and Q: The Enterprise is once again challenged by the dangerous and powerful 'Q', who offers Riker god-like powers in exchange for Riker's membership in the 'Q' continuum.
A nervous man claims he has arrived to visit his wife for their wedding anniversary but what's in his luggage may spell the end of his trip. A drug delivery is intercepted. (S15, ep 7)
A man from Amsterdam says he's here for a holiday, but his suspicious travel itinerary through China and an empty suitcase seem to tell a very different story. (S15, ep 8)
Steven Dean Gordon: After Jarrae Estepp's body was found at a recycling centre, police discovered that her murderer, Steven Dean Gordon, had killed before. (S8, ep 10)
Peter Wrighton: The story of the senseless killing of 83-year-old Peter Wrighton, who was targeted by Alexander Palmer while out walking his dog in 2017.
Strange New World: On an Earth-like planet, the crew become infected with powerful hallucinogens. Can Archer save them before the ensuing paranoia causes a tragedy?
